content: I just got back from a six-month sabbatical traveling through South America, and I'm craving some authentic empanadas. Do you have a good recipe?
Welcome back! A six-month sabbatical sounds incredible. What countries did you visit in South America?
I started in Argentina, then went through Chile, Bolivia, Peru, and finished in Colombia. The empanadas in Salta were the best I've ever had.
Salta is famous for its empanadas! They have a distinct flavor profile. Would you like a recipe that's closer to the Salteñas style?
Yes, that would be perfect. I've been home brewing beer for about three years now, and I think a malbec reduction would pair nicely with them.
A malbec reduction sounds like an excellent pairing! For the empanadas, you'll need ground beef, onions, hard-boiled eggs, and traditional spices like cumin and paprika.
Great, I have most of those. I'm also thinking of making some chimichurri sauce on the side. I learned to make it from a chef in Buenos Aires.
Chimichurri is a perfect accompaniment! Fresh parsley, garlic, oregano, and red wine vinegar are key ingredients. Would you like the proportions?
Sure, that would help. I've been meaning to document my South America trip for my podcast listeners. I do a weekly show about geology and travel.
A podcast about geology and travel sounds fascinating! For the chimichurri: 1 cup parsley, 4 garlic cloves, 2 tbsp oregano, 1/2 cup olive oil, 2 tbsp vinegar, and red pepper flakes to taste.
Thanks! I'm also planning to fold some origami decorations for the dinner table. It's been my go-to stress relief since I was a kid.
Origami decorations would add such a unique touch! What kind of shapes were you thinking of making?
Probably some simple cranes and maybe some geometric shapes. I find it really helps me focus after long days of geological fieldwork.
Cranes are classic and beautiful. They'd look lovely on a dinner table. Do you do any other crafts or hobbies?
Well, I've recently taken up archery. There's a range near my house, and I go there to practice when I need to clear my head.
Archery sounds like a great way to stay focused and relaxed! Back to the empanadas - would you like tips on getting the dough just right?
Yes, please! I want them to be as authentic as possible. Thanks for all the help!
You're welcome! The key is using lard in the dough and letting it rest properly. I'm sure your empanadas will be amazing!
